title: Carbon dioxide
summary: The gas released when a carbonate meets an acid, and a product of every complete combustion. One carbon, two oxygens. Its molar mass sums the sourced weights; the two oxygens make it heavier than it looks at a glance.
notes: A covalent molecular compound, not ionic — named with the count prefix 'di-' rather than by charge crossover (a Phase-2 covalent-naming topic).
